questions.specification: A company is designing everyday electronic devices for public and home use that integrate basic input and output systems to improve usability and efficiency.

parts.content:
They start with a digital bathroom scale that uses a strain gauge to sense weight and display results.

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/a3f99728-f2a8-4463-ba0a-83f79939c6fd)

State the input signal produced by a strain gauge.
parts.markscheme: - An analogue sensor. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Outline two roles of the microcontroller inside the digital scale.
parts.markscheme:
- Converts analogue signal from the strain gauge to digital weight data. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Sends data to the LCD for user display. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award <marks>1 mark</marks>per valid point, up to <marks>2 marks</marks>*

parts.content:
To promote hygiene in public bathrooms, the company develops a touchless automatic faucet system using infrared sensing.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/39284989-1133-47c1-a423-fefca71275c5)

Identify the type of output component used to release water.
parts.markscheme: - A solenoid valve. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Explain how the electronic system ensures water only flows when hands are detected.
parts.markscheme:
- The IR sensor detects reflected IR light from nearby hands. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- It sends a digital signal to the controller. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- The controller activates the solenoid valve to release water. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award <marks>1 mark</marks> per valid explanation, up to <marks>3 marks</marks>*

parts.content:
In a final product concept, they create a kitchen appliance that uses a capacitive touch panel and piezo buzzer to improve safety and control.

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/90de92c6-a05c-40ae-baf0-cc674c42c4a1)

Describe how the capacitive touch panel and piezo buzzer work together to improve user experience.
parts.markscheme:
- Touch panel allows buttonless control for easier cleaning. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Touch input is detected via change in capacitance. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Piezo buzzer gives audio feedback for each input. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Users can confirm actions through sound without looking. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award <marks>1 mark</marks> per valid explanation, up to <marks>4 marks</marks>.*

parts.content: Evaluate the advantages and limitations of using capacitive sensors and piezo buzzers in high-use home appliances.
parts.markscheme:
- Advantage: Touch input is durable and sealed against moisture. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Advantage: Buzzer alerts help users with visual impairments. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Limitation: Capacitive sensors may misfire with wet fingers. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Limitation: Buzzers may not be heard in noisy environments. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Advantage: Low maintenance and fewer moving parts. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Limitation: Lacks haptic feedback of physical buttons. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award <marks>1 mark</marks> per well-explained point, up to <marks>6 marks</marks>.*

questions.specification: How does a digital system process and use information?

options.content: By using binary logic to execute tasks
By representing data as discrete binary states (0s and 1s) and manipulating them using logic gates
By processing information as continuously varying analogue signals
By amplifying input signals without converting them into digital data

questions.specification:
What does this diagram most likely represent in an electronic system?

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/plots/f7e41a88-810d-466d-a2e5-78549e85f12d.png)

options.markscheme: This would typically represent the physical arrangement of support elements in a building or structure, not an electronic system.

options.content: A diagram showing mechanical gear ratios
options.markscheme: This would be used for mechanical systems like transmissions or machinery to show how power is transferred through gears.
A flowchart uses blocks and decision diamonds to show the logic flow of a program or process, rather than the physical connections of components.

options.content: A schematic showing electronic component connections
options.markscheme: A circuit schematic uses standardized symbols (like those for batteries, resistors, and lamps) to show how electronic components are interconnected in a system.

questions.specification:
In the sensor circuit shown below, why is it critical that the operational amplifier possesses a very high input impedance?

![Sensor Circuit](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/ramen-ramen-ib-physics-new-289ef55e-566f-4bdc-bcf3-a9ee7012186f-1772167695732/854f053e-d073-4600-9dd3-b1ea2733b270.jpeg)

options.content: To ensure the output voltage is always inverted relative to the reference voltage
To prevent the amplifier from drawing current and changing the voltage at the potential divider junction
options.markscheme: Ideal operational amplifiers have infinite input impedance. This is essential in sensor circuits so that the amplifier does not "load" the potential divider; if current were drawn into the op-amp, the voltage at the junction would drop, leading to inaccurate sensing and switching.

options.content: To allow the motor to draw its required operating current directly from the non-inverting input
To increase the sensitivity of the LDR by boosting the voltage across the 10kΩ resistor

questions.specification:
What does the comparison in the image best demonstrate?
![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/f28e1d37-1917-413b-abab-7a79d6ff8d7f)

options.content: Difference between analogue and digital signals

questions.specification: Which symbol is typically used to represent a fixed resistor in an IB circuit diagram?

options.markscheme: In the International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) standards adopted by the IB, a fixed resistor is represented by a rectangle.
A zigzag line is the standard symbol for a resistor in North American (ANSI) standards, but is not the standard symbol used in IB exam papers.
A triangle (often with a line at one vertex) is the standard symbol for a diode.
A circle is typically used as a base symbol for components such as lamps, voltmeters, or ammeters.

parts.content: Describe how an analogue system works using voltage, current, resistance, and other relevant SI units.
parts.markscheme:
- State that analogue systems represent information using signals that are continuously variable. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- State that voltage is measured in volts ($\text{V}$). <marks>1 mark</marks>
- State that current is measured in amperes ($\text{A}$). <marks>1 mark</marks>
- State that resistance is measured in ohms ($\Omega$). <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Identify that the frequency of signals is measured in hertz ($\text{Hz}$). <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Identify that electrical power is measured in watts ($\text{W}$). <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification:
Which concept is best illustrated by this waveform graph?

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/plots/021b761d-ced4-41a3-b4a7-524378d80ee1.png)

options.markscheme: The graph shows a series of pulses with a constant frequency/period but varying pulse widths (duty cycles). This is the definition of pulse-width modulation (PWM).
While the signal is digital and non-continuous, the specific variation in pulse width identifies it as PWM rather than just intermittent transmission.
An analogue signal would vary smoothly in amplitude over time, rather than switching between discrete high and low voltage states.
While PWM is often used within step-down voltage regulators (like buck converters), the waveform itself represents the PWM concept specifically.

parts.content: Describe the four basic elements of an electronic system.
parts.markscheme:
Award **[1]** for each element correctly identified and described.

- **Input**: receives a physical signal or energy from the environment and converts it into an electrical signal (e.g., using a sensor or transducer). <marks>A1</marks>
- **Process**: performs an operation, calculation, or transformation on the electrical signal (e.g., amplification, timing, or logic functions). <marks>A1</marks>
- **Output**: converts the processed electrical signal back into a useful form of energy or information for the user (e.g., using an actuator or display). <marks>A1</marks>
- **Feedback**: returns a portion of the output signal back to the input to control, regulate, or stabilize the system's performance. <marks>A1</marks>

questions.specification: In an electronic control system, an operational amplifier is often used in a comparator configuration. What is the primary purpose of the reference voltage ($$V_{ref}$$) in such a circuit?

options.content: It establishes the specific threshold at which the output signal switches state.
options.markscheme: In a comparator circuit, the operational amplifier compares an input signal (from a sensor) against the reference voltage; the output toggles high or low depending on which input is greater, effectively setting the activation threshold.

options.content: It provides the high-current power required to drive an output transducer directly.
options.markscheme: Operational amplifiers are signal processors and generally lack the current capacity to drive high-load transducers directly; this is typically the role of a relay or transistor.

options.content: It acts as a negative feedback loop to stabilize the voltage gain of the system.
options.markscheme: Negative feedback involves connecting the output back to an input to control gain; a reference voltage is a static or adjustable input used for comparison, not for gain stabilization.

options.content: It converts the analogue sensor data into a 4-bit digital binary signal.
options.markscheme: This describes the function of an analogue-to-digital converter (ADC), whereas a simple comparator only provides a single-bit (high/low) binary output.

questions.specification:
What is the primary role of the **cylindrical components** shown on this electronic circuit board?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXfCsLxzsG6wEqEg6z_rco9sMXxbNCoGOQrUC9dazvfSuBJ8T0N8gJfFNNPnXYb57PM0qlGKJZ8GJyutDiEvExX-HAQ--4KoLwG13v7FXlSHV1OEXFcgcmvoCgulfrOs6rdZVXmT4g?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)

options.content: To store electrical energy briefly and help smooth/filter voltage in the circuit
options.markscheme: Correct: cylindrical components on circuit boards are commonly electrolytic capacitors used for energy storage and power-supply smoothing/filtering.

options.content: To provide long-term backup storage for digital data when the power is off
options.markscheme: Incorrect: long-term data storage is provided by non-volatile memory devices, not electrolytic capacitors.

options.content: To amplify electrical signals like a transistor-based gain stage
options.markscheme: Incorrect: amplification is typically performed by transistors/op-amps, not electrolytic capacitors.

options.content: To act as a protective heat-insulating covering for the board
options.markscheme: Incorrect: heat insulation is a materials/packaging function; these components are part of the circuit’s electrical function.

questions.specification: What is the function of an input device in an electronic system?

options.content: To detect changes in the environment

questions.specification:
What function do the input devices in this smart home setup most likely serve?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXchrmGQwmyMvqy5fciLi3SJvn_jDv8NOPS3PW3hbIrUjUtNVla9FK4w-6Qbh08Dcxq0Cf1FbPYDbuvEofCX-cJpurZJJNnAggeCXfX5kNqq5c4-umTdPqCsKU1z8qRk59kueQdR?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)

options.markscheme: Filtering is a signal processing step that may occur within the device, but it is not the primary function of the input device in the context of the overall smart home system.

options.content: Trigger a response to surrounding change
options.markscheme: Input devices in a smart home setup, such as sensors (motion, light, temperature), are designed to detect changes in the environment and provide data to the processor, which then triggers a programmed response.
This is a function of output devices like monitors or speakers.
This is a physical process performed by actuators or heating elements, not a function of an input device.

parts.content: Outline two reasons why energy-efficient electronic products are important in modern society.
parts.markscheme:
- Identification of environmental benefits, such as a reduced carbon footprint or lower consumption of natural resources <marks>A1</marks>
- Outline of how lower energy demand results in fewer emissions from power plants and reduced environmental impact <marks>A1</marks>
- Identification of economic benefits, such as reduced operating costs for the end-user <marks>A1</marks>
- Outline of how energy-efficient products consume less electricity, leading to financial savings on utility bills over the product's lifespan <marks>A1</marks>

4 marks total

<note>Award [1] for identifying a valid reason and [1] for its outline, up to [4] marks total. Accept other valid points such as regulatory compliance (meeting sustainability standards) or brand reputation (enhanced market appeal).</note>

questions.specification:
What function does the control circuit in this appliance most likely serve?
![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/9e270434-ec67-4891-8d5d-41cc61616aa2)

options.content: To amplify sound signals before output
options.markscheme: This describes an audio amplifier/output stage, not the appliance’s control (process) circuit.
Powering speakers is an output/power-delivery function (e.g., a power amplifier), not the role of a control (process) circuit in an appliance.

options.content: To control and monitor the device's internal processes
options.markscheme: A control circuit is the process/control block: it handles logic and sequencing, processes input signals (e.g., switches/sensors), and controls/monitors internal outputs (e.g., motors, magnetron).

options.content: To detect radiation leakage from the casing
options.markscheme: Radiation leakage detection would be a specific sensing/safety subsystem, not the primary general function of the control (process) circuit shown in the system block diagram.

questions.specification: What should designers consider when creating electronic systems for everyday use?

options.content: If they use wires or wireless modules
Responsible use and improvement of quality of life
Whether they are used in homes or hospitals

questions.specification: How do embedded systems in digital electronics function?

options.content: They execute dedicated tasks via programmed instructions

questions.specification: Which best describes a feedback loop in an electronic system?

options.content: A switch between alternating and direct current
An output that becomes a new input for control
The conversion of digital signals into analogue

questions.specification: A team of university students is building a smart indoor hydroponic garden to optimise plant growth using sensors, actuators, and feedback control. The system includes light and moisture monitoring, an automatic watering mechanism, an LED grow light, and a microcontroller interface.

parts.content:
To monitor moisture levels, they integrated a soil moisture sensor into the system.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/51450335-6400-4e53-9533-3e3cfda4ca5a)

Identify the component shown in **Figure 1** and describe its role in the hydroponic system.
parts.markscheme:
- Capacitive moisture sensor <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Detects soil or substrate moisture to determine irrigation needs <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
When low moisture is detected, a peristaltic pump is activated to deliver water.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/259e6d51-6458-4fa5-8b34-2eef6596949f)

i.) Explain why a peristaltic pump is well suited for hydroponic applications.
parts.markscheme:
- Provides accurate flow control of water/nutrient solutions <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Prevents contamination due to sealed tube design <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: ii.) Outline how the pump is actuated using a transistor-based circuit.
parts.markscheme:
- Microcontroller sends signal to base of transistor <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Transistor acts as a switch to complete pump circuit <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Pump receives power and starts water delivery <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
To track environmental conditions, the students used a compact sensor for temperature and humidity.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/f92e17fc-fe71-4a14-8871-345a74121cff)

Identify two types of data the module in **Figure 3** provides and explain how each is used.
parts.markscheme:
- Temperature: helps avoid overheating in grow area <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Humidity: ensures optimal plant transpiration rates <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Describe how the system (**Figures 1-3**) uses feedback to regulate growing conditions in a closed-loop control system.
parts.markscheme:
- Sensors monitor variables like moisture and temperature/humidity <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Microcontroller evaluates input against ideal thresholds <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Actuators adjust water flow (pump) and lighting (LED grow light) as needed <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Changes are continually re-monitored and adjusted <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Evaluate the benefits and drawbacks of using microcontroller-based systems in smart agriculture.
parts.markscheme:
- Automation reduces human error and maintenance <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Enables precise environmental control for better yields <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Can be expensive and complex to set up initially <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Requires programming and electronic skills <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Using a single, labelled circuit diagram that includes **at least four** different component symbols, explain how electronic components are represented in a circuit diagram and state the function of each component you include. You may choose your components from the following list: resistor, capacitor, diode, transistor, switch, relay, LED, operational amplifier.
parts.markscheme:
- Clear, labelled circuit diagram containing at least four correct component symbols from the given list <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Resistor: controls/limits current <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Capacitor: stores energy/charge <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Diode: allows current in one direction <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Transistor: amplifies or switches current <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Switch: opens/closes circuit <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Relay: uses electromagnetism to switch <marks>1 mark</marks>
- LED: emits light when current flows <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Operational amplifier: boosts/amplifies signal <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Award 1 mark for each correctly labelled and explained component (symbol and function) up to 9 marks maximum. <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification: Which of the following would likely be an input device in an electronic circuit?

What is the main purpose of this digital system?

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/c72c8562-3899-4b04-825f-7c8d5fa4dee0.jpeg)

labeledSubSubTopics.path: A. Design in theory > A3.4 Introduction to electronic systems (HL only) > A3.4.12 Embedded systems communication
labeledSubSubTopics.title: A3.4.12 Embedded systems communication
labeledSubSubTopics.reasoning: The question asks for the purpose of a digital system, and the correct option (C) specifically mentions communication and task performance via embedded systems, which is the core focus of Topic A3.4.12.

labeledSubSubTopics.path: A. Design in theory > A3.4 Introduction to electronic systems (HL only) > A3.4.1 Electronic systems
labeledSubSubTopics.title: A3.4.1 Electronic systems
labeledSubSubTopics.reasoning: The question identifies the subject as a digital system, which falls under the broader category of electronic systems covered in A3.4.1.

options.content: Communicate and perform tasks via embedded systems

options.markscheme: <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification:
What task is the embedded digital system shown in the image performing?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXe0AXF65JKfZmVY1XMbCRicR5rl64vVwlKabQQA9wReScMGbDhHWa4GdEcpfidVJ7R_TfAGtFAscGuNbVVVnW_M1byCsy8qyHxDbr_jTRtavLsNgfEXAhQNimnkSMtUJgDLu75AQw?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)

options.markscheme: This matches the functional role of an embedded digital system (processor/controller): to store, process and communicate data/signals.
A rectifier is an analogue power-conversion component, not the core task of an embedded digital system.
Cooling is performed by an output device (e.g., a fan) and is not the functional task of the embedded digital system itself.
An op-amp is an analogue circuit element; sound generation is not the defining task of an embedded digital system.

questions.specification: A start-up company is designing a smart bathroom ventilation system that automatically adjusts fan speed based on humidity levels and user preferences. The system includes sensors, a microcontroller, actuators, and a user interface.

parts.content:
The designers first selected a suitable input device to monitor changing humidity conditions.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/048cd43e-e238-45c5-983f-3110f8411644)

Identify the type of input device shown in **Figure 1** and its function in the system.
parts.markscheme:
Humidity sensor <marks>1 mark</marks>

Detects moisture levels in the air and sends a digital signal to the microcontroller <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] per correct identification and function, up to [2 max].*

parts.content:
The humidity readings are used to control a motor that adjusts airflow within the space.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/d2d93004-bc6e-4e58-a95f-589b46fa5560)

i.)  Outline one reason why a variable-speed motor improves energy efficiency in this system.
parts.markscheme:
Operates only at the required speed to maintain comfort, reducing energy waste <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for valid explanation.*

parts.content: ii.) Explain how the microcontroller controls the fan motor in response to input from the humidity sensor.
parts.markscheme:
Receives humidity data from the sensor <marks>1 mark</marks>

Uses programmed logic to determine appropriate fan speed <marks>1 mark</marks>

Sends control signals to the motor driver or fan circuit <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] per valid point, up to [3 max].*

parts.content:
To make the system intuitive, the engineers added a touchscreen interface that lets users customize their experience.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/065ead92-4a1c-4cce-909c-0f91ed5423ba)

Describe two ways the touchscreen interface enhances user interaction with the ventilation system.
parts.markscheme:
Allows users to set preferred humidity thresholds <marks>1 mark</marks>

Provides real-time feedback on air quality and fan speed <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] per well-explained interaction benefit, up to [2 max].*

parts.content: Referencing **Figures 1-3**, Discuss how feedback loops are used in this system to maintain optimal humidity.
parts.markscheme:
Sensor constantly monitors humidity levels <marks>1 mark</marks>

Microcontroller compares readings to user-defined threshold <marks>1 mark</marks>

Adjusts fan speed accordingly and re-checks conditions <marks>1 mark</marks>

Ensures stable humidity within desired range <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] per point, up to [4 max].*

parts.content: Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using an automated electronic control system versus a manual switch for this ventilation system.
parts.markscheme:
**Advantages**:

Automated adjustments improve comfort and efficiency <marks>1 mark</marks>

Reduced need for user intervention <marks>1 mark</marks>

Can collect data for future optimization <marks>1 mark</marks> **Disadvantages**:

Higher initial cost due to sensors and interface <marks>1 mark</marks>

System complexity may require maintenance <marks>1 mark</marks>

Power usage from always-on microcontroller <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] per balanced advantage/disadvantage, up to [6 max].*

questions.specification: What does an electronic feedback mechanism do?

options.content: Monitors conditions and adjusts system response

questions.specification: Which of the following is a feature of digital electronic systems?

options.content: They encode information in discrete binary values
They can only function without processors

questions.specification:
What key concept of electronic systems is being illustrated in this diagram?

![Block diagram showing a feedback loop](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/644e58c1-5104-4d4e-93a5-ed9e7548aaa6.jpeg)

options.content: Feedback loop in an electronic system

parts.content: Explain how input devices are used to trigger responses in electronic systems. Give two examples.
parts.markscheme:
- **Describe** how input devices detect changes in physical properties (such as light or temperature) within the environment <marks>A1</marks>

- **State** that these sensors convert physical changes into electrical signals which are then sent to a processing unit to trigger a response <marks>A1</marks>

- **Identify** a first valid example, such as a light-dependent resistor (LDR) being used to trigger a security light <marks>A1</marks>

- **Identify** a second valid example, such as a thermistor used to activate a cooling fan when a temperature threshold is reached <marks>A1</marks>

4 marks total

<note>Award 1 mark for each valid point or example up to a maximum of 4 marks.</note>

questions.specification:
Topic 5.2: Heating effect of electric currents

What is the role of electronic components in a system?
What is the key difference between analogue and digital systems?

options.content: Analogue systems use continuous signals, while digital systems use discrete signals.
options.markscheme: Analogue signals can take any value within a continuous range, whereas digital signals consist of discrete values, typically represented as $0$ and $1$.

options.content: Digital systems only use switches, while analogue systems do not.
Analogue systems are battery-powered, while digital systems are not.

questions.specification: Which of the following is true about an analogue system?

options.content: It stores data as binary digits
It uses a processor to calculate logic operations
It sends signals using light pulses
It uses signals like sine waves that vary continuously

questions.specification: Why are electronic systems represented with circuit diagrams?

options.content: To visually show the connection and function of components

questions.specification:
The microchip of the black box is most likely used to

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXe6VZuDkZ19UILoXyUdI6YXhXQODesmNBfWUhwM4gvdS31qzFluEzneHdQH8wuqjSnElqHSwXB1YCj_DTwY2mHsqBEXBAKhlbEjwiTAi2tHKDd5dgHiCEm1b_mJaG4-WNHrUW_z?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)

options.markscheme: A microchip in this context acts as a processor, taking input data or signals and processing them to produce a corresponding output based on its internal logic.

questions.specification: A company is developing various smart home appliances that rely on electronic systems to optimize energy usage, respond to environmental stimuli, and interact with users efficiently.

parts.content:
**Figure 1** shows a smart home thermostat mounted on a wall that automatically regulates room temperature based on time of day and user preferences


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/39c43c07-dfce-427e-806e-13dd6c282022)

State the type of system (analogue or digital) used in this thermostat.
parts.markscheme: - Digital system <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Outline two advantages of using a digital thermostat compared to an analogue one.
parts.markscheme:
- Digital thermostats offer more precise temperature control. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- They can be integrated with software for remote access or learning behaviour. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
**Figure 2** shows a soil moisture sensor module with an Arduino board connected to a small LCD display.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/c5b83c34-7ba4-4e3d-b398-6a06c00e65cb)

Describe the role of the microcontroller in this system.
parts.markscheme:
- The microcontroller receives input from the moisture sensor. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- It processes the input to determine if action is needed. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- It sends output to the LCD display or an actuator like a water pump. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Compare open-loop and closed-loop systems using this example.
parts.markscheme:
- An open-loop system would water plants without checking soil moisture again. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- A closed-loop system continuously monitors the soil and adjusts watering accordingly. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Closed-loop systems reduce over-watering and increase efficiency. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Open-loop systems are simpler but lack feedback accuracy. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
**Figure 3** shows a diagram of basic electronic components, all connected in a circuit.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/a7d00d10-5951-4267-acdd-575963f4f732)

Explain how the different components in the diagram perform specific roles in the system.
parts.markscheme:
- Resistor limits the current to prevent damage to components. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Capacitor stores and releases electrical energy to smooth out voltage fluctuations. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Transistor acts as a switch or amplifier depending on circuit configuration. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Relay controls high-power devices using a low-power signal from the microcontroller. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Diode ensures current flows in only one direction, protecting circuits. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Each component contributes to system stability, safety, or control. <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification:
This smart thermostat automatically adjusts based on room temperature. This most directly demonstrates:

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/a5ab89df-1d32-4fd8-982f-b64a70911f3e.jpeg)

## Question
What happens during the processing stage of an electronic system?

options.content: A signal is stored for future use
Voltage is converted to mechanical motion
Current flows through the entire system
An input signal is transformed into an output

questions.specification:
The circuit is designed to activate a fan when the temperature exceeds a threshold. What role does the circuit play?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXfbgT_Y8zQbWRCIs9tK1U4eqvIbuHnGeWjIFZpTNA_iHnHS34hjSBQ3SKZz3NvPqYvcDdWdmTkX34SWfvSBjV-LRqJ8TyQE2DNgde5oMpHd1v90EfviT7UFakekR_0Shv3Y6uF-?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)

options.content: Controlling output based on sensor input
options.markscheme: Correct: the circuit uses a temperature sensor input and a threshold condition to switch/activate the fan output (a control function).
Incorrect: audio amplification is unrelated to temperature-threshold fan activation/control.
Incorrect: wireless data synchronisation is not part of a simple temperature-triggered fan control circuit.
Incorrect: the described function is to control a fan based on temperature, not to display readings.

questions.specification: What is the correct sequence for the basic operation of an electronic system?
What feature defines the digital nature of a sensor such as a digital thermometer?

options.content: Reading of continuous values in amperes
Display of values as fluctuating waveforms
Output of a continuous analog voltage proportional to temperature

parts.content:
The table below displays the voltage readings recorded from two different sensors in an automated factory system over a period of $$1.5$$ seconds.

| Time ($$\text{s}$$) | Sensor A ($$\text{V}$$) | Sensor B ($$\text{V}$$) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 0.0 | 0.5 | 0 |
| 0.5 | 1.8 | 0 |
| 1.0 | 3.2 | 5 |
| 1.5 | 4.6 | 5 |

Using the data provided, explain why Sensor A is classified as an analogue component while Sensor B is classified as a digital component.
parts.markscheme:
- **State** that Sensor A is analogue because its output varies continuously (shows a range of intermediate values over time) <marks>1 mark</marks>

- **State** that Sensor B is digital because it consists of only two discrete states (specifically $$0\text{V}$$ and $$5\text{V}$$, representing binary off/on) <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification:
## Question
What are the essential functional blocks of an electronic system?

options.markscheme: In systems theory, particularly in electronic control systems, the standard model consists of an input, a processing unit, an output, and a feedback loop to monitor and adjust the system.
These are specific electronic components (passive and active), but they do not describe the functional architecture of a system.
These are examples of specific input (sensor) and output (motor, LED) devices, but they do not represent the general components of a system model.
These are components of a basic electrical circuit, whereas an electronic system refers to the functional blocks of input, process, and output.

questions.specification: What function do control circuits perform in many electronic devices?

A small product design team is developing a night-light that automatically turns on when the room is dark. The team is prototyping the system using a breadboard, a light-dependent resistor (LDR), and a microcontroller.

parts.content:
The system uses an LDR to detect ambient light levels.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/78742e38-7f05-4f8e-8e27-0343eaf73792)

State the type of signal an LDR provides to the microcontroller.
parts.markscheme: An analogue signal that varies with light intensity. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Explain how the microcontroller interprets the LDR input to activate the night light.
parts.markscheme:
It uses an analogue-to-digital converter (ADC) to measure voltage. <marks>1 mark</marks>

It compares the value to a preset threshold to determine darkness. <marks>1 mark</marks>

If the value is below the threshold, it turns the light on. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] per correct point, up to [3 max].*

parts.content:
The microcontroller turns on an LED strip when the room is dark.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/b1d2a899-4510-4a50-94f4-f196c3ca9ee0)

Identify the type of signal sent from the microcontroller to control the LED strip.
parts.markscheme: A digital output signal. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Outline two safety considerations when connecting a high-brightness LED strip.
parts.markscheme:
Ensure correct voltage supply to avoid overloading components. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Include a resistor or current-limiting driver circuit. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] per valid point, up to [2 max].*

parts.content:
The team wants to upgrade the system to include a motion sensor.



![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/37aac554-e5cd-4e3e-a587-3490cf19300b)

Describe how the motion sensor improves the efficiency of the night-light system.
parts.markscheme:
Prevents the light from turning on unnecessarily when no one is present. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Saves energy by combining motion and light detection logic. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] per explanation point, up to [2 max].*

parts.content: Suggest one other sensor that could enhance the product and justify its use.
parts.markscheme:
Temperature sensor to adjust LED brightness based on room temperature. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Adds comfort by dimming the light if the room is warm and brightening it if cold. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for a relevant sensor and [1] for a valid justification, up to [2 max].*

questions.specification: Which describes the function of an output device?

options.markscheme: This describes the function of a secondary storage device.
This describes the function of an input device, specifically a sensor.
This is a specific example of a physical effect caused by an actuator, but it does not represent the general function of all output devices.

options.content: Responds to a stimulus by performing a task.
options.markscheme: This correctly identifies the role of an output device, particularly an actuator in a control system, which performs a physical action or task based on processed data.

topic.title: A3.4 Introduction to electronic systems (HL only)
